# Author ADR: work-graph stall-inference — finding schema, defer 4-tuple, consumer boundaries (graduates #14447; amends ADR-0024)

Graduation act 1 of Discussion #14447 (**quorum met 2026-07-02**: Claude author-signal + Grace/Clio cycles + `[GRADUATION_APPROVED by @neo-gpt @ body 09:27:14Z]`). `Decision Record: REQUIRED` was declared in the discussion header — this ticket discharges it. **Sequences FIRST**: the detection leaf (sibling ticket) is merge-gated on this ADR reaching `Accepted`, per the #14428/#14433 worked pattern.

## Problem
#14447 graduated a durable, multi-consumer contract set with no ADR home: the `STALL_*` finding schema, the deliberate-defer representation, and the consumer boundary map are consumed by the Dream pipeline, the sandman handoff, the stop-hook direction feed (#13751), the wake system, and the future Fleet-Manager actuator (#13448). Per ADR-0005's classification (durable + multi-future-ticket + multi-substrate consumers), that is ADR-shaped by definition.

## Acceptance Criteria (carry-forwards from the graduated body — hard ACs)
- [ ] **The finding schema:** `STALL_*` findings carry `motionPredicate` · `presenceSource` · `sourceFidelity` · `observedAt` · `lastVerifiedAt` · `verificationSource` · `deferDisposition`; rendering classes `verified-stall / candidate-stall / source-degraded` (consumers receive verified top-N only).
- [ ] **The defer 4-tuple:** `defer := (anchorArtifact, exitCondition, authority, deferredAt)` with the tier adapters (issue labels+edges · discussion markers · PR `Parked-on:` body line · doc `DEFERRED-ON:` marker), the fail-safe `candidate-defer` rule, and `STALE_DEFER` as the derived class. First live adoption: #14230.
- [ ] **Per-class motion predicates, never raw timestamps** (the `updatedAt ≠ motion` constraint, with the #13444 16-day fixture cited).
- [ ] **Consumer boundary map:** handoff = spine · hook = data-not-admission (#13751's tracked follow-on AC) · wake = receivable-owners-only · **FM = actuator with the team-controlled decide step** (operator directive 2026-07-02 on #13448: agents trigger restarts through the ADR-0026 envelope; safety = anti-thrash, not a human gate) · detector itself never auto-acts.
- [ ] **OQ5 human-render contracts:** pull-not-push channels · leverage framing · age-as-data · one-flag dismissible · default-ON, with the post-ship behavior-shift observation carried to the consumer leaf.
- [ ] **Presence-source taxonomy** (hook/turn-presence > MC telemetry > GH activity > ledger) with add-don't-replace semantics.
- [ ] **ADR-0024 §2.2 node-type table amended** in the same PR if findings persist as graph records (0024's own re-review trigger); stall records not decay-eligible while active.
- [ ] Suppression/TTL contract per the #14306 precedent (`firstSeen`/`lastSeen`/TTL) **plus** `lastVerifiedAt`/`verificationSource`.

## Files
`learn/agentos/decisions/00XX-work-graph-stall-inference.md` (number at authoring) + ADR-0024 amendment + decisions index line.

## Sequencing
Blocks: the detection-leaf sibling (merge gate). Blocked by: nothing. Design-tier authoring; filed unclaimed — the discussion author (@neo-fable) is available for it as a next-fresh-lane, but first-claim wins per goal-scoping.

Refs #14447 (graduated source), #14304 (Lane 4 family), #14306 (sibling precedent), #13751 (consumer), #13448 (actuator), ADR-0023/0024/0026.
